item.title: Israel’s Latest Genocide Is Against the Shias of Lebanon. Why Is the World Silent?
item.description: Do you want to know what total impunity actually looks like? Look at Israel. It got away with a genocide in Gaza, and now it’s unleashing a similar genocidal campaign against Shia communities in southern Lebanon. Right now, hundreds of thousands of Shia civilians are being displaced, bombed, and…
item.author: Mehdi from Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Do you want to know what total impunity actually looks like? Look at Israel. It got away with a genocide in Gaza, and now it’s unleashing a similar genocidal campaign against Shia communities in southern Lebanon.

Right now, hundreds of thousands of Shia civilians are being displaced, bombed, and forced to flee. Their villages are emptied. Their homes are reduced to rubble. And Israel’s defense minister says they cannot return.

Where is the anger in the West, or in the Global South, or even in the Muslim world? Where are the emergency summits, the outraged headlines, the moral indignation that we saw when, for example, ISIS was carrying out a genocide against the Yazidis in Iraq and Syria?

Think I’m being hyperbolic? First, consider the relevant sections of the 1948 Genocide Convention, which defines genocide as “acts committed with intent to destroy, in whole or in part” a “religious group,” including “killing members of the group” and “deliberately inflicting on the group conditions of life calculated to bring about its physical destruction in whole or in part.”

Next, consider the recent reporting from both the New York Times and Drop Site News, which reveal how Israeli forces have issued evacuation orders across large swaths of southern Lebanon, explicitly targeting Shia communities who make up the majority of the local population.

Wouldn’t this be labeled “ethnic cleansing” if it were any other part of the world? Here is an entire population, an entire religious group, being targeted, displaced, and massacred simply because of who they are and where they live. The current Israeli approach to the Shias of southern Lebanon is collective, indiscriminate, and chillingly familiar: a community of innocents conflated with a security threat. “Every home in southern Lebanon, the Shia homes, are command and control centers,” an Israeli military spokesman told LBC radio in the UK last month, without offering a shred of evidence.

But it gets even worse.

The Israeli military isn’t just forcing Lebanese Shias from their homes and towns in the south; it is reportedly pressuring Lebanese Christians not to provide refuge to their displaced Shia neighbors. “Over the past two weeks,” reports the Times, “Israeli military officials have called leaders of at least eight villages and told them to expel Shiites who had sought refuge in their communities, municipal officials and local Christian, Druse and Shiite leaders said in interviews.”

Let that sink in. In a fragile, multi-faith country, scarred by decades of civil strife and sectarian violence, an invading foreign military is ordering one religious group to close its doors to another.

How is this not setting off alarm bells across the world? Because history offers us only dark precedents. Ethnic cleansing is hardly ever a single, discrete act; it is often a long and vicious process. Nor should we describe what is happening in southern Lebanon as only ethnic cleansing. The Israeli-American historian Omer Bartov, a leading scholar of genocide, has warned that ethnic cleansing is not always distinct from genocide and “can morph into genocide.” Gregory Stanton, the founder of Genocide Watch, goes further, saying “the term ‘ethnic cleansing’ is used as a euphemism for genocide.”

Of course, defenders of Israeli policy will point to the security threat from Hezbollah, the Shia militant group that operates out of southern Lebanon. And yes, Hezbollah exists. Yes, it is armed. Yes, it fires rockets at Israel. But here is the question that too many in the West refuse to consider: since when does the presence of a militia group justify the collective punishment of an entire religious population?

I was born and raised a Shia Muslim. My entire life, I have seen Shias occupy a peculiar place in the political imagination of both the East and the West: rendered invisible but also relentlessly demonized. In Israeli political and media discourse, in particular, Shias are portrayed not as innocent civilians or an oft-oppressed minority community but only as extensions of an expansionist Iran, as armed proxies, as violent threats. For years, Israeli leaders have sought to build a Sunni alliance against Shia actors, speaking conspiratorially of a ‘Shia axis’ or ‘Shia crescent,’ and exploiting sectarian divisions among their Muslim adversaries in a classic divide-and-rule strategy.

I still remember interviewing Naftali Bennett – then-Israel’s education minister, and later prime minister – almost a decade ago for Al Jazeera English, and how startled I was when he told me: “If the Arab countries, the Sunni countries, don’t want to be butchered by their Shia neighbor, they ought to cooperate with us… We need to work together against the big menace.”

In recent years, the Israeli press has gone into anti-Shia overdrive, publishing article after article warning of the “Iranian regime’s dream of establishing a Shi’ite caliphate,” Iran’s “goal for Shia world domination,” and even a “Shi’ite apocalypse.” Last month, after the US-Israeli war on Iran began, one of Israel’s leading scholars of Islamic studies published an op-ed describing the conflict as a “severe blow to Shi’ite Islam” and “marking the beginning of the decline of Shi’ism.”

Nor is this anti-Shia animus and genocidal rhetoric confined only to Israel. It has been echoed and enabled here in the United States. Secretary of State Marco Rubio falsely claims Iran is run by “radical Shia clerics with an apocalyptic vision of the future.” Fellow Republican hawk Senator Lindsey Graham once falsely declared that “the religious doctrine of the Shias in charge compel them to kill all the Jews; it’s a commandment from God.” He also compared Shia Muslims to Adolf Hitler and “religious Nazis.”

These are hardly fringe right-wing voices. These are senior US government officials not only using sectarian bigotry to justify their latest illegal war in the Middle East but also helping to launder it into mainstream discourse.

Remember: When you dehumanize a group long enough, you make their pain and suffering easier to ignore and their displacement and destruction easier to justify.

So when Shia families in southern Lebanon are driven from their homes, when they are denied refuge, when they are targeted and bombarded, it doesn’t trigger the necessary shock or disgust. There is no Yazidi moment here, no global outcry, no talk of “never again.” Just a collective shrug and then the constant and cynical recitation of “Israel’s right to defend itself.”

History – including Jewish history – tells us what happens when entire faith groups are smeared as collectively suspect, and when their neighbors are warned against offering them refuge. We know how that story ends. And yet, here we are, watching it unfold in real time.

So we cannot afford to look away. If the West truly believes in universal human rights, then those rights must apply to Shia Muslims in southern Lebanon as much as they do to Jews in Israel, Yazidis in Iraq, or Ukrainians in the Donbas. And if the Muslim world is capable of recognizing genocide in one context, it must also be willing to recognize its warning signs in another.

Otherwise, our principles are not principles at all. They are merely ethnic and tribal preferences.

As a Shia Muslim, I for one am tired of my faith community being ignored and erased, both by the West and the Muslim world. What’s happening in southern Lebanon to the Shias is not just displacement, not just ethnic cleansing, it is the start of yet another Israeli genocide.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 US Supreme Leader Threatens Death to Iran
item.description: On this day in 1917, the US officially entered World War I when Congress granted President Woodrow Wilson’s request for a declaration of war on Germany. The first US troops would reach France at the end of June. Ultimately, more than 2 million would be deployed to Europe, with 53,000 killed in…
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1917, the US officially entered World War I when Congress granted President Woodrow Wilson’s request for a declaration of war on Germany. The first US troops would reach France at the end of June. Ultimately, more than 2 million would be deployed to Europe, with 53,000 killed in battle.

Happy Monday, all. It’s Swin and Andrew again. Our president celebrated Easter by threatening some “fuckin’” war crimes on Sunday morning, and then seemingly trolled Muslims in the Middle East by praising “Allah” while issuing a threat to kill a ton of Iranian civilians. We sincerely hope you found a better, more humane way to mark the holiday, if you observe it. But with each passing week of this war, Donald Trump reveals himself to be a real-life President Greg Stillson, but with less dignity, somehow. Now, speaking of those potential cataclysmic war crimes…

In today’s ‘First Draft,’ Trump is history’s dumbest madman, Governor Janet Mills is skipping the first Maine Democratic Senate debate against Graham Platner, and NBC is the latest news organization to ask a Democrat if they will dare go on… Hasan Piker’s show.

‘I Am Blowing Up Everything’

The nominal leader of the free world – already a month into an illegal war of aggression that he started – keeps making terrorist threats against the Iranian people. This isn’t empty, lefty rhetoric hyperbole about an out-of-control madman.

Even a brief analysis of Donald Trump’s intimidation tactics these past few days would show that what he’s threatening is not the kind of war that vaguely adheres to any rules or laws. He’s making openly terroristic threats: I will do whatever it takes, however lawless, to achieve my mission; do as I say, or the moms and dads and kids who have nothing to do with this will die horribly.

If this weren’t the American commander-in-chief, almost everyone would be able to see this for what it is. For decades, US politicians and media lost their collective minds over somebody in Iran saying, “Death to America.” What President Trump is doing and propagandizing now makes that three-word catchphrase sound like a schoolyard taunt.

Trump is threatening to commit heinous war crimes if Iran doesn’t open up the Strait of Hormuz – the crucial waterway that Iran has closed since the start of the war, blocking the flow of oil and throttling the global economy. In his primetime address to the nation last Wednesday, Trump argued that other countries should go deal with it, telling them: “Go to the strait and just take it.” By Sunday, he was back to begging Iran to reopen it by threatening to bomb civilians and civilian infrastructure.

“Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran,” Trump posted Sunday on Truth Social. “There will be nothing like it!!! Open the Fuckin’ Strait, you crazy bastards, or you’ll be living in Hell – JUST WATCH! Praise be to Allah.” After that, Trump spoke with a bunch of reporters to expand his threats.

“If they don’t make a deal and fast, I’m considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil,” Trump told Fox. He told Axios, “If they don’t make a deal, I am blowing up everything over there.” Speaking with ABC, Trump said, “If it happens, it happens. And if it doesn’t, we’re blowing up the whole country. We’re blowing up, as I said, it’s going to be bridge day, and it’s going to be power plant day in the country of Iran.”

Over the weekend, several administration officials and close Trump advisers told us that it may not matter if Trump actually is doing his apocalyptic saber-rattling as a negotiating or pressure tactic. Several of these advisers said their fear is that no matter what his intentions are, he could easily end up pushing himself into a bigger, longer war and ground invasion of Iran.

“Escalating to deescalate only works as a strategy if you stop escalating,” one senior Trump appointee bemoans.

If anything, legacy media in this country have dramatically undersold the titanic depravity of what President Trump is doing. The media is covering his social media threats as if he is simply moving a perfunctory deadline back 24 hours, from Monday to Tuesday, but what Trump is discussing is his own pledge to pointlessly commit war crimes against Iran’s civilians – who knows how many? Millions? Tens of millions? – by a certain time. That the American commander-in-chief is doing this in broad daylight is a scandal of monumental proportions.

We should all treat it as such.

And he’s doing it all while trying to have it both ways: He still (laughably) wants to be known as a “peacemaker” and a dealmaker, even as he inches dangerously close to orchestrating a total war and grand-scale killing field in the Middle East.

This isn’t a negotiating tactic. It’s certainly not Madman Theory. It’s just that America is ruled by the cruelest of madmen.

To the Gen Z consumers of this newsletter: You may not realize it, but it is not normal in recent US history for the president of the United States to so routinely threaten to commit explicit war crimes against countless civilians abroad. Even for somewhat recent presidents who have committed a number of them, they did not gleefully announce to the world that they were willing to attack civilian targets, and willing to kill a possibly limitless number of people in a war of aggression.

But for so many Zoomer voters, Trump is most of what they know. In their lifetime, he is what a median, mainstream Republican policymaker is – and this is just what they do now. In stated policy goals, there is scant difference between Trump and his government and the brutal foreign despots that the State Department pretends to be appalled by. They not only sound but behave like nihilistic serial killers. They treat war like ‘Grand Theft Auto’ gameplay.

During his first term, President Trump threatened to attack cultural sites in Iran – an obvious war crime. As violent and as tense as things were at the time, it quickly turned into an empty threat. Swin recalls several high-ranking Trump appointees and aides telling him over the course of that week some version of: That is not gonna happen. People would resign over this, and we can talk him out of it, if it ever gets that far.

But that was 2020. All of those people are gone now, and threatening and perpetrating explosive war crimes is just standard Trump administration policy these days. The widespread expectation among administration brass and their underlings is: If or when Trump decides to order up a bunch more war crimes on Iranian civilian targets, the government and military are going to go along, either enthusiastically or simply dutifully. The Trump-Vance administration is expecting very few, if any, resignations over such a thing.

And according to sources with direct knowledge of the matter, the position in the White House and the Pentagon is that if Trump and his lieutenants demand something that sounds violently illegal, the lawyers in those buildings “are obligated to get to a Yes,” one senior US official tells us. The ones who would vigorously say “no, you can’t do this” have largely been purged or left.

Any shred of moral responsibility or queasiness with slaughter that accidentally popped up every once in a while during Trump’s disastrous first term has been replaced this time around with either genuine or performed bloodlust, both in domestic and foreign affairs.

And that reality is coming to horrific fruition during Trump’s illegal war on Iran.

🇮🇷 Iran War Updates

Death tolls:

Iran: Iran’s deputy health minister told Al Jazeera that at least 1,937 people had been killed in US-Israeli attacks. Iranian officials said last month that nearly 20,000 people had been injured.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 1,460 people, including at least 129 children, in Lebanon, according to yesterday’s figures from the country’s Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 33 people, including 10 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks. The death toll includes four people killed in an Iranian strike today on a residential building in Haifa.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war news…

Retaliation vow: Iran said it would respond to attacks on infrastructure by targeting US-owned or -related facilities. The HQ of Kuwait Petroleum was hit.

IRGC intel head killed: Iran confirmed that Majid Khademi, the head of intelligence for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, was killed in an attack claimed by Israel.

At least 34 killed in 24 hours: US-Israeli strikes across Iran overnight into Monday killed at least 34 people, including six children, Al Jazeera reported, citing Iranian state media. The strikes also targeted Tehran’s Sharif University of Technology, causing extensive damage.

Strait promise: Iran said the Strait of Hormuz would never return to pre-war conditions, especially for the US and Israel. Oil markets opened to rising prices.

Late talks: Axios reported Sunday evening that the US, Iran, and regional mediators are holding “last-ditch” talks on a potential 45-day ceasefire and a possible end to the war. But were these real or just more pre-Monday market manipulation attempts from Trump and co?

Arrests made: US agents detained the alleged niece and grand-niece of Qassem Soleimani, the Iranian commander assassinated by the US in 2020, after their green cards were revoked. Soleimani’s daughter in Iran says the Iranian pair in the US aren’t related to her late father.

Lebanon strikes: A 4-year-old child was among at least 11 people killed in Israeli attacks. Aid groups reported increasing worries for civilians displaced by strikes.

🗞️ What You Need to Know

Health rumors: The White House countered speculation that Trump was in the hospital, saying the 79-year-old was “working nonstop in the White House,” before the president was seen going to his Virginia golf club.

Ballroom blitz: Trump asked an appeals court to allow construction of his grotesque White House addition to resume for security reasons, claiming work done on his orders had left an “open and exposed Executive Mansion.”

Escape from reality: Trump’s budget proposal includes $152 million for year one of rebuilding Alcatraz “as a state-of-the-art secure prison.” The project would cost $2 billion. California Governor Gavin Newsom called the request “stupid.”

Self-censor: The US Holocaust Museum quietly removed online materials about American racism and the fragility of democracy, Politico reported.

ICE time: Musing about running for president, Democratic Arizona senator Ruben Gallego appeared to retreat from his stance that ICE should be “torn down.” He told NBC: “We do need to reform it. We need to right-size it.”

What debate?: Maine Governor Janet Mills dropped out of the first debate of her Democratic US Senate primary against Graham Platner, citing a ‘scheduling conflict.’ The moderator said 900 people were set to attend. Mills trails badly in most polls.

🧠 Pop Quiz!

Donald Trump’s golf club in Virginia was famously discovered to be home to a fake Time magazine cover touting his greatness. What other Trump-ordered fake at the course created headlines in 2015?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

🚨 Don’t Miss It!

Have questions on Trump’s DERANGED Iran threats? Join Mehdi and Prem TODAY at 11am ET, 8am PT, or 4pm GMT, for Zeteo’s ‘Ask the Editor.”

🌏 Anywhere But America

🇵🇸 Don’t forget Gaza: Israel again launched airstrikes in Gaza, killing at least six Palestinians since early Sunday.

🇧🇩 Measles outbreak: At least 98 children in Bangladesh are estimated to have died from measles in recent weeks, pushing Prime Minister Tarique Rahman to launch an emergency vaccination campaign.

🇳🇬 Church attack: Five civilians were killed, and 31 hostages were rescued following an attack on a church in Nigeria’s Kaduna state during an Easter service on Sunday.

🇮🇹 Missing at sea: At least 71 migrants died when their boat capsized, survivors said after Italy rescued 32 others and recovered two bodies in the Mediterranean this weekend.

🙄 WTF?!

“Someone asked me who is the leader of the Democratic Party? I said that’s TDS, Trump derangement syndrome.”

Fetterman has gone from regurgitating Republican talking points on Fox to just spewing nonsense on Fox.

🧠 Trivia answer: A historic plaque marking a civil war battle that never happened.

📺 Sunday Show Roundup

Asked on NBC’s ‘Meet the Press’ if he regretted going on Hasan Piker’s show, Rep. Ro Khanna said he’d “go again” and rejected centrist calls for Democrats to break ties with the streamer.

Republicans resorted to word salads as they attempted to defend the prospect of US group troops in Iran. On ABC’s ‘This Week,’ Rep. Mike Turner failed to give a straight answer on whether ground troops are needed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, cycling through Trump talking points to deflect from the question.

Arguably, even more alarming is how Trump’s party is standing behind his escalation of threats against Iran. GOP House Intelligence chair Rick Crawford said on Fox that Iranians shouldn’t “toy” with Trump, given what he did to Venezuela, adding that “he’s not going to be dissuaded.”

“I’m a yes,” swing-district GOP Rep. Mike Lawler told NBC’s Kristen Welker on his vote to approve billions of additional funding for Trump’s war on Iran, even as the war grows increasingly unpopular among Americans.

🗓️ Mark Your Calendars

Monday, April 6: Trump is expected to hold a press conference with members of the military at 1:00pm EST, after the US rescued a missing airman from Iran.

Tuesday, April 7: Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy is set to begin testifying in his appeal over his campaign finance conviction. Meanwhile, Vice President JD Vance is scheduled to begin a visit to Hungary ahead of its election. Plus, voters in Georgia’s 14th Congressional District will vote in a runoff to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene.

Friday, April 10: The US Court of International Trade will hear several lawsuits challenging the legality of Trump’s latest tariffs. Plus,theArtemis II crew is currently scheduled to return to Earth after a 10-day trip around the moon.

ICYMI From Zeteo

Zeteo’s Martin Pengelly, Akshay Gokul, and Alexa Cohen contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from ‘First Draft’ by going to ‘manage subscription’ in Substack, scrolling down, and toggling the button for ‘First Draft’ off.

The first US troops would reach France at the end of June. Ultimately, more than 2 million would be deployed to Europe, with 53,000 killed in…
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo

item.title: Mehdi Takes Your Questions on Trump's Deranged Iran Threats and More: *MONDAY, 11 ET*
item.description: You’re invited to another episode of ‘Ask the Editor!’ Join Mehdi and Prem for a live Q&A on Monday at 11am ET, 8am PT, or 4pm GMT to break down the latest stories. Subscribers can join the conversation on Zeteo.com, the Substack app, or YouTube. Zeteo’s editor-in-chief will unpack the latest…
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
You’re invited to another episode of ‘Ask the Editor!’

Join Mehdi and Prem for a live Q&A on Monday at 11am ET, 8am PT, or 4pm GMT to break down the latest stories. Subscribers can join the conversation on Zeteo.com, the Substack app, or YouTube.

Zeteo’s editor-in-chief will unpack the latest news on the war on Iran, Trump’s insane threat, and anything else you’re asking about.

Don’t miss it. Tune into ‘Ask the Editor’ below:

If you support the work we do, consider becoming a paid subscriber. A donation would also go a long way in supporting our mission of independent, accountability journalism.

Catch up on more from Zeteo:

item.title: Trump Threatens War Crimes, Bizarrely Praises ‘Allah’ on Easter Sunday
item.description: US President Donald Trump kicked off Easter Sunday by dropping an F-bomb and rage-posting on social media, threatening apocalyptic war crimes in Iran as part of the illegal war he started – while bizarrely praising “Allah.” “Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in…
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
US President Donald Trump kicked off Easter Sunday by dropping an F-bomb and rage-posting on social media, threatening apocalyptic war crimes in Iran as part of the illegal war he started – while bizarrely praising “Allah.”

“Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran,” Trump posted on Truth Social. “There will be nothing like it!!! Open the Fuckin’ Strait, you crazy bastards, or you’ll be living in Hell – JUST WATCH! Praise be to Allah.”

For those keeping track, in his primetime address to the nation last week, Trump insisted that reopening the Strait of Hormuz – which Iran has effectively closed since the start of the war, blocking the flow of oil – should be other nations’ responsibility. Now, he’s threatening to carry out war crimes and destroy Iran’s civilian infrastructure if its regime doesn’t reopen the strait.

Sunday’s disturbing insanity is unfortunately what we have grown to expect from the sitting president of the United States, who the entirety of the Republican Party elite insists is well.

White House officials did not immediately reply to Zeteo’s query about whether this was an appropriate Easter Sunday message to the nation. Other current and former Trump administration officials had initial reactions ranging from “LMAO” to casual exasperation.

Early on in the conflict, Zeteo dubbed this the worst-run war in US history. As evidenced on this Easter Sunday, President Trump and his party keep reinforcing and inflaming that diagnosis.

The Iranian embassy in Thailand responded to Trump’s message on Twitter, referencing the president’s pledge to bomb Iran to the Stone Age: “Judging by how POTUS swears like a teenager, it seems the US has reached the Stone Age sooner than expected.”

A half hour before Trump issued his latest threat, Pope Leo XIV posted his own (much more appropriate) Easter message – a plea for peace.

“In the light of #Easter, let us allow ourselves to be amazed by Christ!” the Pope wrote on Twitter. “Let us allow our hearts to be transformed by his immense love for us! Let those who have weapons lay them down! Let those who have the power to unleash wars choose peace! Not a peace imposed by force, but through dialogue! Not with the desire to dominate others, but to encounter them!”

If only Trump was listening.

Later Sunday morning, Trump told Fox: “If they don't make a deal and fast, I'm considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil.”

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: This Week in Palestine: Israeli Lawmakers Cheer the Death Penalty for Palestinians and ICE Abducts Palestinian Gre…
item.description: Another Palestinian dies in Israeli custody. Another Palestinian green card holder is detained by ICE. And Israel passes another draconian law targeting only Palestinians, and Israeli lawmakers celebrate with…champagne. It goes without saying that it’s been another depressing week in Palestine. We…
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Another Palestinian dies in Israeli custody. Another Palestinian green card holder is detained by ICE. And Israel passes another draconian law targeting only Palestinians, and Israeli lawmakers celebrate with…champagne.

It goes without saying that it’s been another depressing week in Palestine.

We have all those stories and more below, but a quick note first: For now, we’re sending ‘This Week in Palestine’ to all Zeteo subscribers, but that won’t always be the case. To ensure you get the newsletter every week, go to ‘manage subscription’ in your Substack account, scroll down, and toggle on the button for ‘This Week in Palestine’. Please make sure you’re apaid Zeteo subscriber so you can read past the paywall.

Now, here’s another troubling ‘This Week in Palestine’:

Saturday, March 28 – Another Palestinian Dies in Israeli custody

Marwan Herzallah, a 54-year-old man from Nablus, died in Israeli custody. He’s one of more than 100 Palestinian detainees who have died in Israeli prisons or detention camps since Oct. 7 (89 have been identified), according to the Palestinian Prisoners’ Society.

Herzallah had been in detention since early January on “incitement” charges. He had lost a leg in 1995 after being shot by Israeli forces.

Sunday, March 29 –Israel Blocks Jerusalem Cardinal From Holy Site on Palm Sunday

Israel blocked the top Roman Catholic official in Jerusalem from marking Palm Sunday in the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Christianity’s holiest site, and where many believe Jesus was crucified and resurrected. After outcry, including by the Trump administration, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu reversed the decision for the rest of Holy Week.

Israel has closed Holy sites in Jerusalem’s Old City since the beginning of the war on Iran. Notably, the US did not express outrage over the closure of the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound to Muslim worshippers on Eid.

Monday, March 30 – Israeli Lawmakers Approve ‘War Crime Against the Palestinian People’

The Israeli Knesset passed a law that will make the death penalty the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of acts of “terrorism” with the “intent to deny the existence of the State of Israel.”

The draconian law, which in practice will only affect Palestinians, was widely condemned by the UN, Middle Eastern countries, and many Western governments (the US, however, said that it “respects Israel’s sovereign right to determine its own laws”). The Palestinian Authority called the law a “war crime against the Palestinian people.”

Israel’s ultranationalist National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir celebrated with champagne and a noose pin on his lapel, which he’s been wearing as he championed the law in the Knesset.

Tuesday, March 31 –Lazzarini Calls for Probe into Killings of UNRWA Staff

Philippe Lazzarini marked the end of his tenure as the head of the UN Palestinian refugee agency (UNRWA) by calling for a UN investigation into Israel’s killing of more than 390 UNRWA employees in Gaza. "I believe that we need to have a panel – a high-level panel of experts to look into the killing of our staff,” he said.

Wednesday, April 1 –Israeli Soldiers Force Palestinians to Open Shops During General Strike

Israeli forces reportedly forced Palestinian shop owners in the town of Anata, outside Jerusalem’s Old City, to open their shops during a general strike called by Palestinians to protest against Israel’s draconian death penalty law.

Palestinians in major cities across the occupied West Bank marched against the law, with some reports of Israeli forces firing tear gas and rubber-coated bullets at protesters.

Thursday, April 2 –Outrage After ICE Arrests Palestinian Muslim Community Leader

The Islamic Society of Milwaukee (ISM), Wisconsin’s biggest mosque, announced its president, Salah Sarsour, had been abducted by Trump immigration authorities earlier in the week.

Sarsour, who grew up in the occupied West Bank, has been a lawful permanent resident in the US for over three decades, according to ISM.

His attorney told a local newspaper that ICE is holding Sarsour using the same provision in immigration law that the Trump administration has cited to detain others for their pro-Palestine speech, claiming the 53-year-old “poses an adverse consequence to foreign policy considerations.” The administration claims Sarsour lied on his green card application about his conviction as a child in an Israeli military court. Israel has long been accused of abusing detainees to extract confessions. Sarsour’s lawyer said the US government has known about the conviction since the 1990s.

Friday, April 3 – France Arrests Pro-Palestine European Lawmaker

A day after French police arrested French European Parliament member Rima Hassan on suspicion of “apology for terrorism,” the left-wing politician said she was facing “political, judicial, and media persecution” because she has spoken out for Palestine

The arrest, authorities said, stemmed from a since-deleted Twitter post referencing Kozo Okamoto, who took part in an attack at Israel’s Ben Gurion airport in 1972 that killed 26 people.

Hassan’s attorney said the arrest was illegal because she should have immunity as a European member of Parliament.

Miss last week’s edition? Catch up here. Plus, check out more recent essays and interviews from Zeteo:

item.title: ‘I Feel More In Danger Than Ever Before’: Palestinian Activist Speaks Out After Foiled Murder Plot
item.description: A 26-year-old man’s alleged assasination attempt against Palestinian-American activist Nerdeen Kiswani was foiled. But, she tells Prem, she is still facing threats as we speak. Kiswani, co-founder of the organization Within Our Lifetime, has been active in Palestinian human rights advocacy for…
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
A 26-year-old man’s alleged assasination attempt against Palestinian-American activist Nerdeen Kiswani was foiled. But, she tells Prem, she is still facing threats as we speak.

Kiswani, co-founder of the organization Within Our Lifetime, has been active in Palestinian human rights advocacy for years and has been the target of increased harassment over the past two-and-a-half years during Israel’s genocide in Gaza. It’s led to her suing Betar USA, one of several anti-speech organizations that have directed threats toward people in the United States – especially Palestinians – for speaking out against Israel’s genocide. The threats culminated in last week’s attempt to firebomb her home.

Though the plot was foiled, she tells Prem, “I actually feel more in danger than I ever have before.”

Her alleged would-be assailant, Alexander Heifler, was a member of an offshoot of the violent Jewish Defense League organization. His group boasted of working with Betar. Kiswani says groups like Betar have attached “bounties” to her, encouraging people to give her “beepers” (in reference to Israel’s terrorist attack on Lebanon in 2024, using pagers).

Kiswani says threats have continued since the attack. “Tons of Zionist organizations are saying ‘better luck next time,” she says. “It feels like they’re still getting support from lawmakers, government officials, emboldening people to really take the next step and finish the job without getting caught next time.”

Watch Kiswani talk about the plot against her and her family, how she felt about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s response to it, and what she makes of racist Republican Rep. Randy Fine’s vicious campaign against her.

We are removing the paywall so you can watch this interview in full. Please subscribeto avoid paywalls in the future – and to help us do more of this work, and to enable as many people as possible to read and watch it all, too.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: BREAKING: US Fighter Jet Shot Down, Iran Says
item.description: Iran says it has shot down a US F-15 fighter jet. Videos circulating online indicate there’s an ongoing US rescue mission underway to recover the two pilots. Iranian state media has apparently issued a public call for residents to help locate and capture the pilots, with a reward offered for anyone…
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Iran says it has shot down a US F-15 fighter jet. Videos circulating online indicate there’s an ongoing US rescue mission underway to recover the two pilots.

Iranian state media has apparently issued a public call for residents to help locate and capture the pilots, with a reward offered for anyone who helps recover them alive.

By Friday morning, there was a mad dash inside the Trump administration to figure out what was going on and the whereabouts of the American crew, following the launch of a search and rescue operation. The Pentagon did not immediately confirm or deny the Iranian accounts, but sources in the Department of Defense and White House tell Zeteo that they are treating the situation as very real.

One senior Trump administration official – who is among the many on Team Trump who quietly view the war as a disaster that, if prolonged, could bring down the administration – bemoans that a potential hostage situation is one of the scenarios that various officials have feared for weeks: a situation that could inspire President Donald Trump to escalate further instead of attempting to wind down the American war effort.

Even as Trump assures the American people that he wishes to be done with his Middle East “excursion” soon, he has repeatedly warmed to (only to quickly then cool on) the idea of sending in ground troops, an Iran invasion for which his administration has indeed very much prepared. Several senior US officials who spoke to Zeteo believe that a particularly aggressive move by the Iranians, or other factors that Trump may read as a personal humiliation, could easily nudge him back in the direction of extending his war or invading.

The news of the downed jet came a day after the Trump administration attacked civilian infrastructure in Iran – blowing up a bridge, killing eight people and wounding at least 95 – and bragged about it on social media. Trump pledged to carry out more war crimes: “Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!”

As news developed about Iran downing a US fighter jet, Trump posted gleefully on social media about the thought of taking over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has effectively shut down the crucial waterway, stopping the flow of oil and throttling the global economy. US allies have, thus far, refused to join in on any mission to clear the strait – which would be exceedingly dangerous.

Trump suggested in his primetime address on Wednesday evening that he would leave it to other countries to open the strait. Yet, on Friday morning, he posted, “With a little more time, we can easily OPEN THE HORMUZ STRAIT, TAKE THE OIL, & MAKE A FORTUNE. IT WOULD BE A “GUSHER” FOR THE WORLD??? President DONALD J. TRUMP”

This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: Donald Trump, Warmonger-in-Chief
item.description: On this day in 1948, President Harry Truman formally created the Marshall Plan, providing aid to European Cold War allies. In a move the current president might question, Truman kept his name off the plan, instead using bipartisan respect for Secretary of State George Marshall to smooth its passage…
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1948, President Harry Truman formally created the Marshall Plan, providing aid to European Cold War allies. In a move the current president might question, Truman kept his name off the plan, instead using bipartisan respect for Secretary of State George Marshall to smooth its passage through Congress.

Good morning, Mehdi here. Thank goodness it’s Friday? Normally, yes. Normally, you can relax a bit at the end of the week. But during this war, Fridays bring an ominous feeling to all of us here at Team Zeteo. Because the weekends, with markets closed, are when Trump likes to escalate. Will this be the weekend, the long weekend, that Trump decides to send ground forces into what could become a “bloodbath” for US troops? We’ll be watching.

In today’s ‘First Draft,’ warmonger Trump picks the military-industrial complex over his own working-class base; women don’t seem to last long in the Trump administration; Tiger Woods calls Trump from the scene of his DUI car crash; and Hamas refuses to disarm until Israel withdraws its forces from Gaza.

Warfare Not Day Care, Says Donald

Do you remember when private citizen Donald Trump and then 2016 presidential candidate Donald Trump pretended to be anti-war? When he said US wars in the Middle East had “bled our country dry,” and railed against “wasting lives and money” overseas? When he repeatedly insisted that those billions should be used at home to “rebuild the USA”?

Today, President Donald Trump is spending billions of dollars every single day on his own illegal and catastrophic war of choice in the Middle East. One online tracker puts the direct cost of his Iran war at almost $40 billion in less than 40 days. The Trump administration is already seeking more than $200 billion in additional funding from Congress for the Department of Defense, to pay for the war and to restock munitions.

But that’s not all. Later today, the president of the United States will unveil his 2027 White House budget, which is expected to feature a massive and unprecedented escalation in defense spending across the board.

The expected 50% jump in Department of Defense spending, from $1 trillion to $1.5 trillion, would mean the US would be spending roughly three times as much on defense as all of the European members of NATO combined. It would be the biggest increase in US defense spending since the Korean War and would exceed the biggest year-on-year increases in Pentagon expenditures under Republican hawks like Ronald Reagan (around 20% in 1983) and George W Bush (around 15% in 2003).

But wait… there’s more.

Guess how Trump, our supposedly populist president, plans to pay for this ginormous hike in defense spending, which is predicted to add nearly $6 trillion to the national debt over the next decade once interest costs are included? By cutting federal spending on healthcare and social safety net programs for needy Americans.

You could not make it up.

Here he is speaking at the White House on Wednesday:

Yes, he actually said it. “The United States can’t take care of day care… We’re fighting wars… It’s not possible for us to take care of day care, Medicaid, Medicare, all of these individual things… We have to take care of one thing: military protection.”

If we had a half-decent Democratic Party, or a half-decent mainstream media, this clip would be all over every single one of your social feeds and be appearing nonstop in every ad break you see on television.

If we had an actual opposition in this country, they’d also be calling this president a war criminal. Because that’s what he is. And he is cutting public services here in the United States to pay for his widespread destruction of civilian infrastructure in Iran. After pledging to “bomb Iran back to the Stone Ages” in his White House address on Wednesday, Trump bombed a highway bridge near Tehran on Thursday, killing eight people according to Iranian state media, then bragging on Truth Social that there would be “much more to follow” if the Iranian government didn’t agree to a deal with the US.

The US military “hasn’t even started destroying what’s left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!” Trump reiterated last night on his social media site.

This is the literal definition of terrorism: inflicting violence against civilian targets to achieve political ends. Israel and the US are engaged in undeniable acts of terrorism against the people of Iran and Lebanon. Don’t take my word for it. Ask two of Trump’s own high-profile supporters from 2024, podcasters Joe Rogan and Theo Von, who have both soured on Trump’s warmongering. This was their exchange on Thursday’s episode of ‘The Joe Rogan Experience’:

Got that? When Rogan said that “supposedly they’re trying to stop the terrorists,” Von responded: “That’s crazy though if you’re the fucking terrorists… If you wanna stop ‘em, fucking stand in front of the fucking mirror.”

Von seemed to be referring to the Israelis in Lebanon, but his words apply to the US in Iran, too. As Trump prepares to escalate his illegal war and expand his war budget, and as he is accused by more than 100 leading experts in international law of violating the UN Charter and committing “potential war crimes,” I ask again: Where are the Democrats? Where is the mainstream media? Why is it left to a podcaster and comedian who happily attended Trump’s inauguration to call out US/Israeli “fucking” terrorism in the Middle East?

🇮🇷 Iran War Updates

Death tolls:

Iran: Iran’s deputy health minister told Al Jazeera that at least 1,937 people had been killed in US-Israeli attacks. Iranian officials said earlier this month that nearly 20,000 people had been injured.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 1,345 people, including at least 125 children, in Lebanon, according to yesterday’s figures from the country’s Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 29 people, including 10 Israeli soldiers, have been killed and thousands injured in Iranian and Hezbollah attacks.

Elsewhere in the region: Dozens of others have been killed across the region, including four Palestinian women in the occupied West Bank.

And in more Iran war news…

Tehran’s warning: Iran targeted a water desalination plant and oil refinery in Kuwait and a gas plant in the UAE, Kuwaiti and Emirati officials said, as Tehran warned it would attack regional energy infrastructure and telecom companies with US shareholders if America attacked its bridges, energy plants, and other civilian infrastructure. Iran, without providing evidence, later blamed the attacks on the Kuwait desalination and oil plants on Israel.

Strait talks: A coalition of 40 countries, formed by the UK, discussed actions for reopening the Strait of Hormuz and released a statement calling on Iran to stop blocking the route. No countries signaled willingness to use military force.

Army change: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth pushed out the Army chief of staff, General Randy George. Outlets, including CBS, reported Hegseth wanted someone who would implement the Trump administration’s vision for the army.

Weaponry intact: Iran maintains about half its missile launchers and thousands of drones, CNN reported, contradicting remarks on Wednesday, in which Trump said Tehran’s missile and drone capabilities were “dramatically curtailed.”

New regulations: Iran and Oman are drafting a protocol for supervising the Strait of Hormuz after the war. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said the plan was not intended to impose restrictions, but to “ensure safe passage.”

‘You’re Fired’ (Females)

Pam Bondi is out. Kristi Noem is long gone. Tulsi Gabbard or Lori Chavez-DeRemer could be next.

Donald “you’re fired” Trump is taking a scythe to his kakistocratic cabinet.

But, hmm, what do they all have in common? ‘Never Trumper’ pundit Bill Kristol seems to have put his finger on it.

🗞️ What You Need to Know

Parting words: Pam Bondi, who reportedly “begged” to keep her job as attorney general, said she would move to “an important private sector role I am thrilled about.” Reported reasons for firing included that Trump’s perceived enemies have shrugged off DOJ probes and the Epstein files continue to fester. Todd Blanche, once Trump’s lawyer, will be acting AG.

Shutdown punt: DHS remains partially closed after hardline House Republicans refused to pass a Senate funding plan Speaker Mike Johnson agreed to pursue. Reports said no fix will be possible until April 13, when the House returns in full.

Executive time: Trump said he would sign an order to pay all DHS employees. He did not say where he would find the money.

AI alarm: Maine is set to ban new data centers, the Wall Street Journal said. The aim is to buy time to study the effects on the environment and the electric grid.

🧠 Pop Quiz!

Kristi Noem, who Donald Trump fired as homeland security secretary, famously shot a dog named Cricket. What did Pam Bondi, fired as attorney general, do to a dog named Master Tank?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

🌏 Anywhere But America

🇵🇸 Don’t forget Gaza: Hamas said it was not willing to comply with Israel’s demand for disarmament as part of ceasefire talks and would only discuss the option after the so-called “Board of Peace” addresses key demands, including ending Israeli ceasefire violations and creating a plan for withdrawing Israeli forces from Gaza.

🇫🇷 France ‘intimidating’ Palestine supporters: France detained European Parliament member Rima Hassan on suspicion of “apology for terrorism” over a since-deleted Twitter post referencing Kozo Okamoto, who took part in an attack at Israel’s Ben Gurion airport in 1972 that killed 26 people. “The French police and justice system are being used to intimidate those who support the Palestinian people,” said Sophia Chikirou, a member of Hassan’s La France Insoumise (LFI) party.

🇧🇫 War crimes report: Human Rights Watch found Burkina Faso’s military responsible for war crimes, including the ethnic cleansing of the Fulani group, amid the killing of thousands and displacement of millions in the past few years.

🇲🇲 Myanmar cements military rule: Myanmar’s parliament chose Min Aung Hlaing, a general who helped oust Aung San Suu Kyi’s civilian government five years ago, as its new president – cementing the military’s power after the country held its first election since the coup, an election observers say was neither free nor fair.

🇷🇺🇨🇺 Second tanker: Russia’s energy minister said it plans to send another oil shipment to Cuba, as the island nation struggles under US pressure. The first tanker, allowed through by the Trump administration, arrived in Cuba this week.

💬 Quote Unquote

“You better just be breaking your back and working hard. Maybe get yet another job.”

That’s what New Jersey GOP Rep. Jeff Van Drew had to say to Americans struggling to get by. It’s almost like Republicans want to lose the midterms.

😵💫 WTF?!

“I was just talking with the president,” Tiger Woods can be seen and heard telling cops prior to his recent DUI arrest in this video released by TMZ. Yeah, he called Trump from the scene of his crash. Totally normal thing to do.

🧠 Trivia answer: She tried to take him from his owner — a 4-year-old boy.

📖 Weekend Reads

What the Birthright Case Is Really About

Trump went to the Supreme Court this week to hear oral arguments in his attempt to end birthright citizenship – a move Adam Serwer of The Atlantic says would create a “permanent, stateless, hereditary subclass” of some 6 million people by 2050. Serwer, who coined the phrase “the cruelty is the point” to describe Trump’s politics, walks readers through the history of birthright citizenship and how Trump is seeking to make “America white again.”

Israel’s death penalty law is not about executing Palestinians

This week, Israel passed a one-sided law making the death penalty the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis. Experts warn that the law will be weaponized to execute Palestinians however Israeli officials see fit. For Al Jazeera, Mariam Barghouti argues that the death penalty for Palestinians “did not begin with this law.”

Trump’s Secret Wars on the World Keep Expanding

Crunching the numbers, The Intercept found that in his time in office, Trump has dragged the US into at least 20 military interventions, armed conflicts, and wars. Like most things Trump says, his claim to be the “peace president” is just another lie.

ICYMI From Zeteo

Zeteo’s Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from ‘First Draft’ by going to ‘manage subscription’ in Substack, scrolling down, and toggling the button for ‘First Draft’ off.
